prompt: Katsushika Hokusai ukiyo-e woodblock print style. A lone traveler descends a steep mountain pass toward a glowing valley far below. The path zigzags downward in bold compositional diagonals — the traveler has clearly followed the steepest available slope at each turn. A small hollow in the hillside sits to one side, bypassed — the path curves around it and continues down toward the deeper valley floor which glows with warm light. Flat unmodulated areas of indigo, ivory, and pale ochre — absolutely no photographic shading, no gradients, no blending. Visible woodgrain texture throughout the image. Stark figure-ground contrast. Bold outlines. The traveler is tiny against the mountain. No text. No labels. Edo period aesthetic, traditional Japanese composition.
tradition_approach: Hokusai compositional diagonals, flat unmodulated color fields in indigo and ochre, visible woodgrain texture, stark figure-ground contrast — absolutely no photographic shading or perspective gradients.
